summary: Scrutiny failed because the new session_input_callback persistence logic does not preserve upstream provenance semantics when callback output contains items whose values appear in both prior history and the new turn. In that case the Rust code always classifies the matching item as fresh input first, so it can re-persist old history or drop the real new item, which means VAL-CORE-005 is not fully closed despite the added happy-path coverage.

issues.description: The callback-to-session persistence classifier matches callback output against new-turn items by value before it checks history, so duplicate-valued items cannot be attributed correctly. If history already contains an item equal to a new item and the callback returns the history copy (or reorders duplicates), the Rust runner will persist that history item as if it were new input, diverging from the upstream identity-plus-frequency semantics for session_input_callback provenance.
issues.evidence: crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:32-60 classifies combined callback items by searching remaining_new_items first and only then remaining_history. The upstream contract explicitly requires separating callback output against copied history and new-input pools using identity first and frequency second (reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/run_internal/session_persistence.py:70-75,122-146; reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/src/runner/sessionPersistence.ts:366-381). The added Rust test only covers a transformed new item, not duplicate-valued history/new-item collisions (crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs:168-194).

codeReview.summary: Most of the added coverage matches the feature brief, but the core session_input_callback implementation still misses an upstream edge case around duplicate-valued items, so the feature should not pass scrutiny.

issues.description: Callback output items are attributed to the new turn purely by value equality before checking history, which breaks upstream provenance rules when history and new input contain equal items.

commands.observation: Reviewed the committed feature scope, touched files, and commit metadata.
Reviewed the full implementation diff for the feature's changed files.

commands.command: python3 - <<'PY' ... print numbered excerpts from crates/agents-core/src/internal/session_persistence.rs ... PY
commands.observation: Captured exact line evidence for the new callback classification logic and the added Rust regression test.

commands.observation: Captured upstream source-of-truth evidence showing identity-plus-frequency matching requirements for callback provenance.
